key_insight: This thematic review shows that elderly care OR research has advanced from static scheduling models to dynamic, AI-enabled decision support, but still rarely links operational gains to patient health outcomes.
engineering_relevance: The paper is relevant for designing smarter healthcare systems that integrate routing, staffing, polypharmacy, and care-transition decisions into patient-centered operational frameworks.
method: Reviewed 30 seminal studies in elderly care OR
Grouped the literature into home healthcare, polypharmacy, and chronotherapy
Compared model evolution from deterministic to stochastic and AI-integrated approaches
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a thematic review of 30 seminal studies and identifies a broad methodological shift, but it does not present new experimental results or outcome metrics.
watch_item: A major limitation is the translational gap between process optimization and measurable clinical outcomes, especially across hospital-to-community care transitions.
paper_title: From Operations to Elderly Care Outcomes: A Thematic Review of Industrial Engineering and Decision-Support Approaches
